specific_change: No changes recommended. Each tier was evaluated against its healthy operating bands and SLA targets (99.5% availability, P95 < 500ms). Compute CPU p95 peaks at 78% with comfortable headroom, memory stays under 72%, application P95 latency at 194.6ms max is well within the 500ms target. Database query P95 latency maxes at 113.4ms with a strong cache hit ratio (≥0.92). Redis cache hit ratio holds at 88-96% with memory usage peaking at 73.8%. Network error rate never exceeds 1%. No cross-tier correlations were flagged. Rightsizing, replica adjustment, and scaling policy changes were all considered and ruled out as the current configuration is well-balanced for cost, performance, and reliability.

telemetry_observations: Compute CPU p95 ranges 26.2–78.0%, with a median of 63.3% — healthy utilization without saturation.
Application P95 latency max is 194.6ms, well under the 500ms SLA target (p50 at 129.7ms).
Memory p95 peaks at 71.4% with a mean of 55.3%, leaving adequate headroom.
Database query P95 latency max is 113.4ms; db_cache_hit_ratio stays ≥0.92 across the window.
Cache hit ratio mean is 0.921 with memory usage peaking at 73.8% — no eviction pressure.
Network error rate p95 is 0.009 (0.9%), max 0.01 (1.0%) — no reliability concern.
Network P95 latency max is 78.2ms, contributing modestly to overall application latency.
infrastructure_context: ASG configured min=4/max=8 with target_tracking; desired=4 is sufficient given CPU and latency profiles.
db.r6g.large with 200GB storage and 1 replica is appropriately sized for observed connection counts (max 90 p95) and query latency.
4× cache.r6g.large Redis nodes with 3600s TTL sustain a 92%+ hit ratio with moderate memory usage.
ALB using least_outstanding_requests algorithm — appropriate for the workload distribution.

reasoning: All four tiers were systematically checked against healthy operating bands. Compute CPU utilization sits in the 40-65% median range with peaks under 80%, which is the sweet spot for target-tracking ASGs — high enough to avoid waste, low enough to absorb bursts. The application P95 latency of ~180ms at the 95th percentile leaves a 64% margin to the 500ms SLA. Database and cache metrics are similarly well-positioned: query latencies are low, hit ratios are high, and connection counts show no pooling pressure. The empty cross-tier correlation array confirms no hidden coupling issues. At $3,900/month the cost posture is proportionate to a tier-2 internal tool with these resource levels.
